items.title: Pauline Hanson under fire after migration and NDIS comments

items.content: Claims from Pauline Hanson that Muslims are rorting the NDIS and that removing the white Australia policy has created migration problems have come under fire.

items.title: NSW woman arrested over movements of Dezi Freeman – as it happened
items.standfirst: This blog is now closedGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastPauline Hanson has been called “un-Australian” over her appearance on a podcast with far-right UK activist, Tommy Robinson.The Greens senator Sarah Hanson-Young told ABC RN Hanson must return to Australia and apologise.This is so appalling, and frankly, it’s unAustralian.To go overseas to hang out with a criminal thug … to be laughing on his show about multiculturalism back here in Australia – which are our communities, Australian citizens, and the people who make this country great, Pauline Hanson is the most unAustralian politician in the parliament, and she should come home, face the music, and apologise.I‘m not sure where Ms Hanson is getting her figures from, but they’ve never been provided to me as the minister for disability and the minister for the NDIS.I suspect they don’t exist.I’m loath to respond to a podcast between Ms Hanson and this convicted criminal, who frankly has been disowned by so many leading figures on the right. Continue reading...
items.content: This blog is now closedGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastPauline Hanson has been called “un-Australian” over her appearance on a podcast with far-right UK activist, Tommy Robinson.The Greens senator Sarah Hanson-Young told ABC RN Hanson must return to Australia and apologise.This is so appalling, and frankly, it’s unAustralian.To go overseas to hang out with a criminal thug … to be laughing on his show about multiculturalism back here in Australia – which are our communities, Australian citizens, and the people who make this country great, Pauline Hanson is the most unAustralian politician in the parliament, and she should come home, face the music, and apologise.I‘m not sure where Ms Hanson is getting her figures from, but they’ve never been provided to me as the minister for disability and the minister for the NDIS.I suspect they don’t exist.I’m loath to respond to a podcast between Ms Hanson and this convicted criminal, who frankly has been disowned by so many leading figures on the right. Continue reading...

items.title: Pauline Hanson laments end of White Australia policy and talks up daughter’s leadership potential
items.standfirst: Appearing on a podcast with the far-right UK activist who goes by the name Tommy Robinson, Hanson makes evidence-free claims about migrantsFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastPauline Hanson has used an interview with the far-right British activist Tommy Robinson to blame the end of the White Australia policy for creating what she sees as problems with migration and said her daughter, Lee Hanson, was the future of One Nation.The One Nation leader, who is visiting the UK for a series of conference appearances and a holiday with her billionaire benefactor Gina Rinehart, appeared on an episode of Robinson’s podcast on Friday, repeating unsubstantiated claims about the spread of sharia law and accusing Muslim communities, without evidence, of rorting the national disability insurance scheme. Continue reading...
items.content: Appearing on a podcast with the far-right UK activist who goes by the name Tommy Robinson, Hanson makes evidence-free claims about migrantsFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastPauline Hanson has used an interview with the far-right British activist Tommy Robinson to blame the end of the White Australia policy for creating what she sees as problems with migration and said her daughter, Lee Hanson, was the future of One Nation.The One Nation leader, who is visiting the UK for a series of conference appearances and a holiday with her billionaire benefactor Gina Rinehart, appeared on an episode of Robinson’s podcast on Friday, repeating unsubstantiated claims about the spread of sharia law and accusing Muslim communities, without evidence, of rorting the national disability insurance scheme. Continue reading...

items.title: One Nation’s economic thought bubbles have little substance – except for one | Greg Jericho
items.standfirst: The idea of a people’s bank has been long floated by the Greens. Anything that reduces the oligopoly power of the big four banks is a good thingFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastBarnaby Joyce is now the One Nation Treasury spokesperson, which serves as a lovely reminder of his triumphant 15 weeks as shadow spokesperson for finance and debt reduction from December 2009 to March 2010. A period during which he went to the National Press Club and confused millions with billions, and talked about “net gross public and private debt” which … is not a thing.But with Pauline Hanson too busy swanning around in the UK meeting with far-right convicted criminals to care about her own constituents, someone has to talk about things that might affect Australians. Continue reading...
items.content: The idea of a people’s bank has been long floated by the Greens. Anything that reduces the oligopoly power of the big four banks is a good thingFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastBarnaby Joyce is now the One Nation Treasury spokesperson, which serves as a lovely reminder of his triumphant 15 weeks as shadow spokesperson for finance and debt reduction from December 2009 to March 2010. A period during which he went to the National Press Club and confused millions with billions, and talked about “net gross public and private debt” which … is not a thing.But with Pauline Hanson too busy swanning around in the UK meeting with far-right convicted criminals to care about her own constituents, someone has to talk about things that might affect Australians. items.title: Life-changing MS drugs to stay on the PBS. So why are others under threat – and how is Trump involved?
items.standfirst: Health minister Mark Butler confirms Ocrevus, Kesimpta and Lemtrada will stay subsidised amid questions about the Pharmaceutical Benefits SchemeFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAustralians living with multiple sclerosis will continue receiving cheaper access to life-changing medical treatments, after an expert advisory panel decided to maintain taxpayer subsidies for key drugs.The health minister, Mark Butler, confirmed on Thursday that the drugs – Ocrevus, Kesimpta and Lemtrada – would not be removed from the Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme, ending uncertainty as manufacturers pushed the government not to reduce the price they paid for drugs. Continue reading...

clusters.title: 'Carry on patriots': Trump officials defend pilot after fighter jet stunt
clusters.neutral_headline: 'Carry on patriots': Trump officials defend pilot after fighter jet stunt

items.title: 'Carry on patriots': Trump officials defend pilot after fighter jet stunt
items.standfirst: Officials, including US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, continue to support low-altitude military flyovers despite growing safety concerns after a jet was filmed buzzing a crowded beach. Trump administration officials have backed the manoeuvre, with the White House posting a cartoon of the stunt along with the words "it's okay to love America". Similar flights have previously come under review but were dismissed by the Pentagon.
items.content: Officials, including US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, continue to support low-altitude military flyovers despite growing safety concerns after a jet was filmed buzzing a crowded beach. Trump administration officials have backed the manoeuvre, with the White House posting a cartoon of the stunt along with the words "it's okay to love America". Similar flights have previously come under review but were dismissed by the Pentagon.

items.standfirst: DJ and music producer Josh Fawaz has updated the song credits on Spotify on his viral track Like a Prayer. Generative AI drums and vocals are now credited.
items.content: DJ and music producer Josh Fawaz has updated the song credits on Spotify on his viral track Like a Prayer. Generative AI drums and vocals are now credited.

clusters.title: Decade-long friendship between Roy Orbison's hit maker and Australian singer
clusters.neutral_headline: Decade-long friendship between Roy Orbison's hit maker and Australian singer

items.title: Decade-long friendship between Roy Orbison's hit maker and Australian singer
items.standfirst: Every Monday morning for the last 14 years, Australian musician Damien Leith has been meeting with the late Joe Melson, the Nashville songwriter responsible for some of Roy Orbison's biggest hits.
items.content: Every Monday morning for the last 14 years, Australian musician Damien Leith has been meeting with the late Joe Melson, the Nashville songwriter responsible for some of Roy Orbison's biggest hits.

clusters.title: Will Ferrell channels Ricky Bobby in TV return
clusters.neutral_headline: Will Ferrell channels Ricky Bobby in TV return

items.title: Will Ferrell channels Ricky Bobby in TV return
items.standfirst: Plus a noir mystery set in outback Coober Pedy, a true crime drama which doesn't exploit its victims, and more new TV to watch this month.
items.content: Plus a noir mystery set in outback Coober Pedy, a true crime drama which doesn't exploit its victims, and more new TV to watch this month.

clusters.title: Drivers wait months for faults to be repaired on EVs built by new brands
clusters.neutral_headline: Drivers wait months for faults to be repaired on EVs built by new brands

items.title: Drivers wait months for faults to be repaired on EVs built by new brands
items.standfirst: As an influx of car brands enters the market, dealers ask for a change to consumer laws to fix a grey area that makes them responsible for warranty repairs.
items.content: As an influx of car brands enters the market, dealers ask for a change to consumer laws to fix a grey area that makes them responsible for warranty repairs.

clusters.title: Uber reverses 'disability tax' after outcry
clusters.neutral_headline: Uber reverses 'disability tax' after outcry

items.title: Uber reverses 'disability tax' after outcry
items.standfirst: Uber has reversed its decision to impose a $5 fee on passengers requiring extra assistance, after advocates and service providers labelled it discriminatory and a "disability tax".
items.content: Uber has reversed its decision to impose a $5 fee on passengers requiring extra assistance, after advocates and service providers labelled it discriminatory and a "disability tax".
